cells.label: masculine
cells.function: masculine gender — noun in -o; agrees with determiner el/los/un (and contractions del/al)

cells.label: feminine
cells.function: feminine gender — noun in -a; agrees with determiner la/las/una

cells.label: plural
cells.function: number=plural — suffix -s (covaries with the English-pivot Plural feature)

conditioning: gender (a fixed lexical property of the noun, exposed by the agreeing determiner) crossed with number (-s); adjectives + articles agree in both

examples: -e and consonant-final nouns (gender not predictable from the ending)
fit_none.note: the detector recovers the two GENDERS (via determiner agreement) and NUMBER (via -s) as separate axes; it does not split the 4 combined gender×number cells, and -e/consonant nouns carry no ending cue — the expected residue

evidence_citations.claim: two noun-ending classes take distinct determiners

evidence_citations.stat: -s co-varies with the projected Number=Plur feature (~0.71)

evidence_citations.claim: gender is a real agreement system, not phrase case-marking
evidence_citations.stat: the gender_or_noun_class switch reads 'gender' (distinct determiners el≠la), unlike tgl's invariant ang

prose: spa has GENDER (two classes) + NUMBER. Gender surfaces as agreement: nouns ending -o take the determiner el (and del/al/los), nouns ending -a take la — distinct determiners on distinct endings is the gender-agreement signature (and distinguishes it from a phrase case-marker like Tagalog's invariant ang). Number is the suffix -s, recovered from its covariation with the English Plural feature. The detector recovers the two genders and the number marker as separate axes; it does not separate the four combined gender×number cells, and -e/consonant-final nouns give no ending cue (the residue). Recommendation: PROMOTE gender (2-class agreement) and number (-s); flag the gender×number cross-product + unpredictable-ending nouns for review.
